title: California Head Injury Lawyer | Frank Penney Injury Law
metaDescription: California head injury lawyer for serious head trauma and TBI victims. $1B+ recovered. Free consultation 24/7. No fee unless we win. Home & hospital visits.
heroSubheadline: Suffered a Serious Head Injury? Get a Free Case Review.
whyText: Serious head injuries can change a life forever — affecting memory, concentration, mood, and the ability to work. Frank Penney Injury Law has 30+ years securing the long-term care and financial support head injury victims need.
citiesTitle: California Head Injury Lawyers Across Northern California
citiesLede: Frank Penney Injury Law represents head injury victims across Sacramento, Roseville, Fairfield, Stockton, Modesto, Chico, and the rest of Northern California. We handle every type of head trauma case — concussions, post-concussive syndrome, TBI (traumatic brain injury), and serious head trauma from car accidents, motorcycle crashes, slip-and-falls, sports incidents, and workplace injuries. The long-term costs of a head injury — ongoing rehabilitation, cognitive therapy, lost earning capacity — often run into the millions, and we fight for the full value of those losses.
serviceDescription: Representing California head injury and TBI victims with $1 Billion+ recovered. We handle concussion, post-concussive syndrome, and traumatic head trauma cases throughout Sacramento, Roseville, Fairfield, Stockton, Modesto, and Chico.

faq.q: What counts as a serious head injury?
faq.a: A 'serious' head injury usually means trauma that produces ongoing cognitive, physical, or emotional symptoms — memory problems, concentration deficits, headaches, mood changes, sleep disturbances, sensitivity to light or sound, or difficulty returning to work. Even injuries that didn't involve loss of consciousness can produce significant long-term effects.

faq.q: What's the difference between a concussion and a more serious head injury?
faq.a: A concussion is a milder form of head trauma, typically with shorter recovery. More severe head injuries can produce longer-lasting cognitive and physical symptoms — sometimes permanent. Modern imaging (MRI, DTI) and neuropsychological testing help document the extent of an injury that may not show up on a standard CT scan.

faq.q: How long do head injury symptoms typically last?
faq.a: Most concussion symptoms resolve within weeks. Post-concussive syndrome can extend symptoms for months or years. More serious head injuries can produce permanent cognitive or behavioral changes. Long-term tracking by neurologists and neuropsychologists is critical both for medical care and for documenting the case.

faq.q: How much is a head injury case worth?
faq.a: Serious head injury cases are among the most valuable in personal injury — settlements and verdicts often range from high six figures to many millions of dollars. Value depends on medical bills, future medical care, lost earning capacity, vocational rehabilitation, and the impact on the victim's daily life and relationships.

faq.q: What evidence do I need to prove a head injury claim?
faq.a: Emergency room and neurology records, MRI/CT/DTI imaging, neuropsychological testing, the treatment timeline, witness accounts of cognitive or behavioral changes, employment records showing the impact on work, and family testimony about changes from the pre-injury baseline. Frank Penney Injury Law works with leading medical experts to fully document the case.

faq.q: How long do I have to file a head injury lawsuit in California?
faq.a: Generally two years from the date of the accident under California's personal injury statute of limitations. The 'discovery rule' may extend the deadline if the head injury wasn't immediately apparent — but that's a difficult exception. Government-defendant cases require a claim within six months.

faq.q: Can I get compensation for cognitive therapy and long-term rehabilitation?
faq.a: Yes. Future medical care — cognitive therapy, occupational therapy, neuropsychological treatment, medications, and specialized rehabilitation programs — is fully recoverable. We work with life-care planners to project decades of future care costs, which often comprise the largest part of a head injury settlement.

faq.q: What if my head injury wasn't diagnosed at the scene?
faq.a: Common — head injuries are often missed initially. Symptoms can develop hours, days, or even weeks after the trauma. Don't let an insurance company use a 'delayed diagnosis' against you. We work with experts who can connect later-diagnosed symptoms back to the original incident.

faq.q: Can I still work after a serious head injury?
faq.a: Many head-injury victims can return to some form of work, but often at reduced capacity, lower pay, or in different roles. A vocational expert can quantify lost earning capacity — often a major component of damages. We don't accept low-ball settlements that ignore long-term work limitations.

faq.q: How much does it cost to hire a head injury lawyer?
faq.a: Frank Penney Injury Law works on a contingency basis. You pay nothing up front and no fee unless we win. Free consultations are available 24/7 by phone at (888) 888-0566 or at any of our 6 California offices. We make home and hospital visits — important for clients who can't easily travel after a head injury.

testimonials.author: Jennifer M.
testimonials.text: The settlement was more than I could have received on my own and the fact that I could hand it all over to him to resolve was without a doubt worth it! It was the best decision I ever made throughout this process.

testimonials.author: Robert S.
testimonials.text: At a time when I was most vulnerable, I received support and understanding. Frank Penney Injury Lawyers provide excellent customer service. I cannot recommend them enough to my friends and family!

testimonials.text: I would highly recommend this firm for those that do not know anything about the procedures that take place after a car injury. They were responsive, accommodating, and I had a good feeling about the firm from the start.
